goals: YouTube videos uploaded from ReelPod Studio have a polished, search-friendly title.
Descriptions include the song concept, mood/genre, and transparent AI model attribution.
Users can review and edit the title and description before confirming the upload.
Model credits are maintained in a single YAML file — easy to update without touching code.

userStories.title: LLM generates a YouTube title and description during orchestration
userStories.description: As a creator, I want the orchestration LLM to produce a YouTube-optimised title and description for my track so that I don't have to write metadata manually before publishing.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `OrchestrationResult` gains two new fields: `youtube_title` (max 100 chars) and `youtube_description` (max 5000 chars).
The `CREATIVE_DIRECTOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` is updated to instruct the LLM to return these two fields in its JSON output.
`youtube_title` is a human-readable string with no underscores and no file-extension
`youtube_description` contains a short paragraph describing the song concept, mood, and
Both fields are validated by Pydantic (non-empty, within length limits).
Existing fields (`song_title`, `audio_prompt`, `image_prompt`, `video_prompt`) are
Backend unit tests cover valid output and the case where the LLM omits these keys.
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: Backend exposes a model-credits YAML and appends it to the description
userStories.description: As a developer, I want model credits defined in a plain YAML file so that I can update the model list without touching Python or TypeScript code.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A new file `backend/config/model_credits.yaml` exists with at least the following
A backend helper reads this file at startup (or lazily) and exposes a function
The `/api/generate` response (or a new `/api/youtube-metadata` endpoint) returns both
If the YAML file is missing or malformed, a clear startup warning is logged and
Backend unit tests cover: valid YAML, missing file, malformed YAML.
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: Queue entries carry YouTube metadata to the frontend
userStories.description: As a creator, I want the generated YouTube title and description to be available in the recording queue so that they can be shown in the preview dialog.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The `QueueEntry` type in `App.tsx` gains optional fields `youtubeTitle: string | null`
When the backend returns `youtube_title` / `youtube_description` (via the
Existing queue entries where orchestration was not used (manual params, no LLM) have
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: Preview/edit dialog before YouTube publish
userStories.description: As a creator, I want to review and optionally edit the YouTube title and description before the video is uploaded so that I can correct any LLM mistakes.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Clicking the "Publish to YouTube" button for a queue entry opens a modal dialog (not a
The dialog has a **Cancel** button (closes without uploading) and a **Publish** button
Field values edited by the user are sent verbatim to `uploadVideoToYouTube` — no
If `youtubeTitle` is empty in the dialog, the Publish button is disabled with a visible
Visually verified in browser: dialog opens, pre-fills correctly, edits are preserved on
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: `publish_to_youtube` MCP tool uses the generated metadata
userStories.description: As an automated agent, I want the `publish_to_youtube` MCP tool to default to the LLM-generated title and description so that publishing via MCP is also SEO-friendly without manual arguments.

acceptanceCriteria.text: When `title` and `description` are omitted from the MCP call, the tool reads
When `title` or `description` are explicitly passed, those values take precedence
If no metadata is available (null), the tool falls back to the filename-derived title
Typecheck / lint passes.

functionalRequirements.description: A `backend/config/model_credits.yaml` file defines model roles, names, and a footer
`build_credits_block()` reads the YAML and returns a formatted plain-text string;
The backend returns `youtube_title` and `youtube_description` to the frontend as part
`QueueEntry` stores `youtubeTitle` and `youtubeDescription` for each completed entry.
A preview/edit modal in the UI lets the user review and optionally modify title and
The `publish_to_youtube` MCP tool defaults to queue-entry metadata when no arguments
